description:
cloudstack_egress_firewall

Creates egress firewall rules for a given network.

argument_reference.name: network_id
argument_reference.description: The network ID for which to create the egress firewall rules. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

argument_reference.name: managed
argument_reference.description: USE WITH CAUTION! If enabled all the egress firewall rules for this network will be managed by this resource. This means it will delete all firewall rules that are not in your config! (defaults false)

argument_reference.name: rule
argument_reference.description: Can be specified multiple times. Each rule block supports fields documented below. If managed = false at least one rule is required! parallelism (Optional) Specifies how much rules will be created or deleted concurrently. (defaults 2) The rule block supports:

argument_reference.name: cidr_list
argument_reference.description: A CIDR list to allow access to the given ports.

argument_reference.name: protocol
argument_reference.description: The name of the protocol to allow. Valid options are: tcp, udp and icmp.

argument_reference.name: icmp_type
argument_reference.description: The ICMP type to allow. This can only be specified if the protocol is ICMP.

argument_reference.name: icmp_code
argument_reference.description: The ICMP code to allow. This can only be specified if the protocol is ICMP.

argument_reference.name: ports
argument_reference.description: List of ports and/or port ranges to allow. This can only be specified if the protocol is TCP or UDP.

attributes_reference.name: id
attributes_reference.description: The network ID for which the egress firewall rules are created.
